chunks.text: FOR Nu HCJDC.12/ ORDER SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE Tعاتطظھنا W٢.N%.165689[201 Ahsan Iftikhar VErSUS Board of Intermediate & Secondary Education, etc 5٢، N 07 ٧F٢ Darg 0] DrUgr Order win sigrulurt oy Jige und ahul ٣٢٥٤٤٤٥٥١٥٨ Procerdlng ٢ ' ١١ ٢ ١ ٦0٧54 gc٤5505٧ل فتعلنا 15.05.2019 Ms Mehmood Ahmad Alvari and Mian Muhammad Sharif, Advocates for petitioner.MT, Mehboob Azhar Sheikh, Advocate for respondent- Board Mrs. Zarish Fatima Assistant Attorney General and Mr Muhammad Arshad Manzur, AAG Through this Constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question order dated 2912 2U18 passed by Discipline Committe Board of Intermediate and Sccundary Education (BISE), Lahore Buard Wniereby Ihe FSult Ine petitioner Ior Secondary School Certificate (Pant I & II), Annual Examination, 2018 has been cancelled and has also called in question urder dated 05.03.2019 passed by Appeal Committee of the Board whereby the afore referred decision has been upheld by dismissing the petitioner appeal The learned counsel for petitioner states that after having passed the afore reterred examination and declaration uf his result, the Board was not vesteu with any authority proceed against the petitioner under Chapter rule sub-rules 0f Board Calendar ( Calendar as the said provision could only be invoked during conduct of examination and that too before rSult has been declared consequently tne impugned orders are claimed to be without jurisdiction

FOR Nu HCJDC.12/ ORDER SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE Tعاتطظھنا W٢.N%.165689[201 Ahsan Iftikhar VErSUS Board of Intermediate & Secondary Education, etc 5٢، N 07 ٧F٢ Darg 0] DrUgr Order win sigrulurt oy Jige und ahul ٣٢٥٤٤٤٥٥١٥٨ Procerdlng ٢ ' ١١ ٢ ١ ٦0٧54 gc٤5505٧ل فتعلنا 15.05.2019 Ms Mehmood Ahmad Alvari and Mian Muhammad Sharif, Advocates for petitioner.MT, Mehboob Azhar Sheikh, Advocate for respondent- Board Mrs. Zarish Fatima Assistant Attorney General and Mr Muhammad Arshad Manzur, AAG Through this Constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question order dated 2912 2U18 passed by Discipline Committe Board of Intermediate and Sccundary Education (BISE), Lahore Buard Wniereby Ihe FSult Ine petitioner Ior Secondary School Certificate (Pant I & II), Annual Examination, 2018 has been cancelled and has also called in question urder dated 05.03.2019 passed by Appeal Committee of the Board whereby the afore referred decision has been upheld by dismissing the petitioner appeal The learned counsel for petitioner states that after having passed the afore reterred examination and declaration uf his result, the Board was not vesteu with any authority proceed against the petitioner under Chapter rule sub-rules 0f Board Calendar ( Calendar as the said provision could only be invoked during conduct of examination and that too before rSult has been declared consequently tne impugned orders are claimed to be without jurisdiction لخۂککلقنشا On the other hand, the learned counsel for Board hus relied upon Chapter 14 rule 14 of the Board Calendar argue that the Board has the power tu quash the result candidate at any time even after it has ben declared; therefore no exception be taken to the impugned orders Heard, record perused ubsurved Ihat the petitioner appeared Secondary School Certificate ( Part-II), Annual Examination, 2OI8 under Roll No222358 and his result '٧5 declared as `pass" and he collectively obtained 1U31 marks out of IOU for both parts uf the said examination and w٥5 awarded Secondary School Certificate Thereatter got admission Goverment College University, Lahore but subsequently, on an anonymous complaint received the Board, result the petitioner W٥5 cancelled afler AISWf S03[3 his physics practical examination were found to have certain discrepancies Moreuver, it was observed that the answer sheets have subsequently interpolated by deletion of wrong answers and correct answers in collusion with tne sub examiner, charge sheet under tne provisions of Conduct of Examinations provided in Chapter 15 rule sub rules & ofthe Calendar was IS3ued t0 the petitiuner requiring him to file reply The said rules are reproduceed below: tundidufe jound guilty o any oy the Jollowing OUEnCeS shall be فثتوع passing Lhat eoشسeon and سفت oPPشفg عل معنفس عتفعفسن beناn inscrting لسە

FOR Nu HCJDC.12/ ORDER SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE Tعاتطظھنا W٢.N%.165689[201 Ahsan Iftikhar VErSUS Board of Intermediate & Secondary Education, etc 5٢، N 07 ٧F٢ Darg 0] DrUgr Order win sigrulurt oy Jige und ahul ٣٢٥٤٤٤٥٥١٥٨ Procerdlng ٢ ' ١١ ٢ ١ ٦0٧54 gc٤5505٧ل فتعلنا 15.05.2019 Ms Mehmood Ahmad Alvari and Mian Muhammad Sharif, Advocates for petitioner.MT, Mehboob Azhar Sheikh, Advocate for respondent- Board Mrs. Zarish Fatima Assistant Attorney General and Mr Muhammad Arshad Manzur, AAG Through this Constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question order dated 2912 2U18 passed by Discipline Committe Board of Intermediate and Sccundary Education (BISE), Lahore Buard Wniereby Ihe FSult Ine petitioner Ior Secondary School Certificate (Pant I & II), Annual Examination, 2018 has been cancelled and has also called in question urder dated 05.03.2019 passed by Appeal Committee of the Board whereby the afore referred decision has been upheld by dismissing the petitioner appeal The learned counsel for petitioner states that after having passed the afore reterred examination and declaration uf his result, the Board was not vesteu with any authority proceed against the petitioner under Chapter rule sub-rules 0f Board Calendar ( Calendar as the said provision could only be invoked during conduct of examination and that too before rSult has been declared consequently tne impugned orders are claimed to be without jurisdiction لخۂککلقنشا On the other hand, the learned counsel for Board hus relied upon Chapter 14 rule 14 of the Board Calendar argue that the Board has the power tu quash the result candidate at any time even after it has ben declared; therefore no exception be taken to the impugned orders Heard, record perused ubsurved Ihat the petitioner appeared Secondary School Certificate ( Part-II), Annual Examination, 2OI8 under Roll No222358 and his result '٧5 declared as `pass" and he collectively obtained 1U31 marks out of IOU for both parts uf the said examination and w٥5 awarded Secondary School Certificate Thereatter got admission Goverment College University, Lahore but subsequently, on an anonymous complaint received the Board, result the petitioner W٥5 cancelled afler AISWf S03[3 his physics practical examination were found to have certain discrepancies Moreuver, it was observed that the answer sheets have subsequently interpolated by deletion of wrong answers and correct answers in collusion with tne sub examiner, charge sheet under tne provisions of Conduct of Examinations provided in Chapter 15 rule sub rules & ofthe Calendar was IS3ued t0 the petitiuner requiring him to file reply The said rules are reproduceed below: tundidufe jound guilty o any oy the Jollowing OUEnCeS shall be فثتوع passing Lhat eoشسeon and سفت oPPشفg عل معنفس عتفعفسن beناn inscrting لسە للخقغظا conuicuIg atempting COIIunICute airccIty or inurrechy wiih an examier with ٧C٧ ilucncing him In Ihe award oymarhs Jorg"& muliloling 0NE٢I٤ 01٤/ w154 lempering wifhi ٥٨} docunell coninecied wifh CtUIIUNOn W٥Ag Miule USe 0y Such docuunenl ubetting comilsston oy such oJfences emphasis supplicdy Ihe Rule of Chapter 15 provides that the candidate, if found guilty of offences Ientioned therein, shall disqualified fT0I11 passing that exumninalion and from appearing in two innediately fullowing examinations, It is observed from the language of the afore referred rule that said provisions could only exercised hefore result examination W٥5 declared as the candidate is to be declared disqualified from passing the said examination and the same could nOt be invoked after result had been declared After declaration of result the petitioner could although proceeded against and result could be quashed under Chapter 14 rule 14 of the Calendar, the said provision to proceed against the petitioner was not mentioned in the charge sheet and hence such powers wru nul actually excrciscU. The relevant portion of the said Chapter is reproduced below: The Board shall hqve the power to quash the resuli 0y cundidafe ٥١١ ٥٧1 qffer hn5 been 02010"80.' ٧ h٤ been uusquatilied Jor msing uofr meuns fn Ihe Examination' Dr Wu misfuae isyoua Il MiS FeSM Or Ir is Jound that Me wasز eligible UDLU Il I٤ CUIUHU Jor uny ofher reuson Ihar may be determined by Ihe Bourd " C٣٧5٧!8

Zarish Fatima Assistant Attorney General and Mr Muhammad Arshad Manzur, AAG Through this Constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question order dated 2912 2U18 passed by Discipline Committe Board of Intermediate and Sccundary Education (BISE), Lahore Buard Wniereby Ihe FSult Ine petitioner Ior Secondary School Certificate (Pant I & II), Annual Examination, 2018 has been cancelled and has also called in question urder dated 05.03.2019 passed by Appeal Committee of the Board whereby the afore referred decision has been upheld by dismissing the petitioner appeal The learned counsel for petitioner states that after having passed the afore reterred examination and declaration uf his result, the Board was not vesteu with any authority proceed against the petitioner under Chapter rule sub-rules 0f Board Calendar ( Calendar as the said provision could only be invoked during conduct of examination and that too before rSult has been declared consequently tne impugned orders are claimed to be without jurisdiction لخۂککلقنشا On the other hand, the learned counsel for Board hus relied upon Chapter 14 rule 14 of the Board Calendar argue that the Board has the power tu quash the result candidate at any time even after it has ben declared; therefore no exception be taken to the impugned orders Heard, record perused ubsurved Ihat the petitioner appeared Secondary School Certificate ( Part-II), Annual Examination, 2OI8 under Roll No222358 and his result '٧5 declared as `pass" and he collectively obtained 1U31 marks out of IOU for both parts uf the said examination and w٥5 awarded Secondary School Certificate Thereatter got admission Goverment College University, Lahore but subsequently, on an anonymous complaint received the Board, result the petitioner W٥5 cancelled afler AISWf S03[3 his physics practical examination were found to have certain discrepancies Moreuver, it was observed that the answer sheets have subsequently interpolated by deletion of wrong answers and correct answers in collusion with tne sub examiner, charge sheet under tne provisions of Conduct of Examinations provided in Chapter 15 rule sub rules & ofthe Calendar was IS3ued t0 the petitiuner requiring him to file reply The said rules are reproduceed below: tundidufe jound guilty o any oy the Jollowing OUEnCeS shall be فثتوع passing Lhat eoشسeon and سفت oPPشفg عل معنفس عتفعفسن beناn inscrting لسە للخقغظا conuicuIg atempting COIIunICute airccIty or inurrechy wiih an examier with ٧C٧ ilucncing him In Ihe award oymarhs Jorg"& muliloling 0NE٢I٤ 01٤/ w154 lempering wifhi ٥٨} docunell coninecied wifh CtUIIUNOn W٥Ag Miule USe 0y Such docuunenl ubetting comilsston oy such oJfences emphasis supplicdy Ihe Rule of Chapter 15 provides that the candidate, if found guilty of offences Ientioned therein, shall disqualified fT0I11 passing that exumninalion and from appearing in two innediately fullowing examinations, It is observed from the language of the afore referred rule that said provisions could only exercised hefore result examination W٥5 declared as the candidate is to be declared disqualified from passing the said examination and the same could nOt be invoked after result had been declared After declaration of result the petitioner could although proceeded against and result could be quashed under Chapter 14 rule 14 of the Calendar, the said provision to proceed against the petitioner was not mentioned in the charge sheet and hence such powers wru nul actually excrciscU. The relevant portion of the said Chapter is reproduced below: The Board shall hqve the power to quash the resuli 0y cundidafe ٥١١ ٥٧1 qffer hn5 been 02010"80.' ٧ h٤ been uusquatilied Jor msing uofr meuns fn Ihe Examination' Dr Wu misfuae isyoua Il MiS FeSM Or Ir is Jound that Me wasز eligible UDLU Il I٤ CUIUHU Jor uny ofher reuson Ihar may be determined by Ihe Bourd " C٣٧5٧!8 للخععسشا Ihe sheet which was in the nature of show- causu nolice issued to Ine petitioner requiring him to file reply should have specifically mentioned the details of allegations and the provisions of Iaw against which the petitioner ؟1'11 required to be proceeded against and in case specifie details were not mentioned the authorities proceeding against the petitioner would be proceeding against him unUcr Inle wTong assumption said violations, Reliance in this regard is placed on Iعطع تظفكل4 M عن Bسt فنه d uleg (PLD 2٥٥5 S.C. 94) and lr ana ٧ظ L سسسعلا ف oس سسك Collecturute Cf Centul Eف Lفتسلس d عشش (NLR 2003 CLJ 830) Non mentioning of Chapter I4 rule 14 in the sheet clearly indicates that the Board did not initiate proceedings against the petitioner under the said rule which power was vested in it fur cancellation quashing of result after its declaration, therefore by passing an order under the said provisions without including the same in the charge sheet would be proceedings wrong assumptions and not sustainable in the eye of as what is nut charged through sheet cannut be proved The time to invoke provision of Chapter 15 Rule sub-rules & had expired when Ine petitioner ٤ result n4s been declared Although grounds refered in the said rule have been made the grounds for proceedings petitioner if show-cause notice under correct provisions of law rules had been issued by specifically mentioning the same but show cause notice having not mentioned provision chapter 14 rule 14 of the calendar was ambiguous, vague charge تلا churge churge ٧٠ ١ could agauinst شحعشا and devoid of power under which the same was issued consequently it not be held that the vested powers had propurly been exercised It is settled by now that WhUr law requires particular to be done in particular manner, the same could only be done in that munner and not otherwise as the said dictate of law could not be treated mere technicality and such aCIIOIl could not be held to be in compliance with legislative intent and not sustain in the eye of law.

Zarish Fatima Assistant Attorney General and Mr Muhammad Arshad Manzur, AAG Through this Constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question order dated 2912 2U18 passed by Discipline Committe Board of Intermediate and Sccundary Education (BISE), Lahore Buard Wniereby Ihe FSult Ine petitioner Ior Secondary School Certificate (Pant I & II), Annual Examination, 2018 has been cancelled and has also called in question urder dated 05.03.2019 passed by Appeal Committee of the Board whereby the afore referred decision has been upheld by dismissing the petitioner appeal The learned counsel for petitioner states that after having passed the afore reterred examination and declaration uf his result, the Board was not vesteu with any authority proceed against the petitioner under Chapter rule sub-rules 0f Board Calendar ( Calendar as the said provision could only be invoked during conduct of examination and that too before rSult has been declared consequently tne impugned orders are claimed to be without jurisdiction لخۂککلقنشا On the other hand, the learned counsel for Board hus relied upon Chapter 14 rule 14 of the Board Calendar argue that the Board has the power tu quash the result candidate at any time even after it has ben declared; therefore no exception be taken to the impugned orders Heard, record perused ubsurved Ihat the petitioner appeared Secondary School Certificate ( Part-II), Annual Examination, 2OI8 under Roll No222358 and his result '٧5 declared as `pass" and he collectively obtained 1U31 marks out of IOU for both parts uf the said examination and w٥5 awarded Secondary School Certificate Thereatter got admission Goverment College University, Lahore but subsequently, on an anonymous complaint received the Board, result the petitioner W٥5 cancelled afler AISWf S03[3 his physics practical examination were found to have certain discrepancies Moreuver, it was observed that the answer sheets have subsequently interpolated by deletion of wrong answers and correct answers in collusion with tne sub examiner, charge sheet under tne provisions of Conduct of Examinations provided in Chapter 15 rule sub rules & ofthe Calendar was IS3ued t0 the petitiuner requiring him to file reply The said rules are reproduceed below: tundidufe jound guilty o any oy the Jollowing OUEnCeS shall be فثتوع passing Lhat eoشسeon and سفت oPPشفg عل معنفس عتفعفسن beناn inscrting لسە للخقغظا conuicuIg atempting COIIunICute airccIty or inurrechy wiih an examier with ٧C٧ ilucncing him In Ihe award oymarhs Jorg"& muliloling 0NE٢I٤ 01٤/ w154 lempering wifhi ٥٨} docunell coninecied wifh CtUIIUNOn W٥Ag Miule USe 0y Such docuunenl ubetting comilsston oy such oJfences emphasis supplicdy Ihe Rule of Chapter 15 provides that the candidate, if found guilty of offences Ientioned therein, shall disqualified fT0I11 passing that exumninalion and from appearing in two innediately fullowing examinations, It is observed from the language of the afore referred rule that said provisions could only exercised hefore result examination W٥5 declared as the candidate is to be declared disqualified from passing the said examination and the same could nOt be invoked after result had been declared After declaration of result the petitioner could although proceeded against and result could be quashed under Chapter 14 rule 14 of the Calendar, the said provision to proceed against the petitioner was not mentioned in the charge sheet and hence such powers wru nul actually excrciscU. The relevant portion of the said Chapter is reproduced below: The Board shall hqve the power to quash the resuli 0y cundidafe ٥١١ ٥٧1 qffer hn5 been 02010"80.' ٧ h٤ been uusquatilied Jor msing uofr meuns fn Ihe Examination' Dr Wu misfuae isyoua Il MiS FeSM Or Ir is Jound that Me wasز eligible UDLU Il I٤ CUIUHU Jor uny ofher reuson Ihar may be determined by Ihe Bourd " C٣٧5٧!8 للخععسشا Ihe sheet which was in the nature of show- causu nolice issued to Ine petitioner requiring him to file reply should have specifically mentioned the details of allegations and the provisions of Iaw against which the petitioner ؟1'11 required to be proceeded against and in case specifie details were not mentioned the authorities proceeding against the petitioner would be proceeding against him unUcr Inle wTong assumption said violations, Reliance in this regard is placed on Iعطع تظفكل4 M عن Bسt فنه d uleg (PLD 2٥٥5 S.C. 94) and lr ana ٧ظ L سسسعلا ف oس سسك Collecturute Cf Centul Eف Lفتسلس d عشش (NLR 2003 CLJ 830) Non mentioning of Chapter I4 rule 14 in the sheet clearly indicates that the Board did not initiate proceedings against the petitioner under the said rule which power was vested in it fur cancellation quashing of result after its declaration, therefore by passing an order under the said provisions without including the same in the charge sheet would be proceedings wrong assumptions and not sustainable in the eye of as what is nut charged through sheet cannut be proved The time to invoke provision of Chapter 15 Rule sub-rules & had expired when Ine petitioner ٤ result n4s been declared Although grounds refered in the said rule have been made the grounds for proceedings petitioner if show-cause notice under correct provisions of law rules had been issued by specifically mentioning the same but show cause notice having not mentioned provision chapter 14 rule 14 of the calendar was ambiguous, vague charge تلا churge churge ٧٠ ١ could agauinst شحعشا and devoid of power under which the same was issued consequently it not be held that the vested powers had propurly been exercised It is settled by now that WhUr law requires particular to be done in particular manner, the same could only be done in that munner and not otherwise as the said dictate of law could not be treated mere technicality and such aCIIOIl could not be held to be in compliance with legislative intent and not sustain in the eye of law. Reliance placed on ا تسططه نسلا سسسسا سلظ مہسلا Nazi فتقسه ف (PLD 2018 S.C. 189) Shمسiس Bibi کلا كسله طنطظ Bt فتسل ف نن (PLD 2016 $.C. 995) and تسفةeس کسسلہ ؟ Hsi mdoheت (2OI4 SCMR IOI5) Consequently it is held that by not issuing sheet by Ientioned Chapter 14 rule 14 uf the Calendar and by mentioning provisions under Chapter 15 rule sub-rules & only, for cancellation quashing of already declared FeSull, the respondents had exercised jurisdiction not vested in themn, therefore, the said exercise of jurisdiction is held to be without lawful authority The learned counsel for Board has stressed that the provision of Chapter 15 Rule not only barred Ihe candidate from passing the relevant examination but it also provides Inut would 8|50 barred from appearing in two subsequent examinations to be held immnediately thereafter therefore 11 15 Ihar Inis provisibn invoked ٤v٤٨ aller passing Ine exumination but am not in agreement with the said argument on the ground that unless the order barring the petitioner from passing relevant examination had been cuuld thing could 4للا charge urgued لخفگعلسشنلا passed at the relevant time before declaration of result, Ihe subsequent order barring the petitioner ITOI appearing In two next examinations could not have been passed especially when provisions under Chapter 14 rule na0 n0l Deen InVUkcu through sheet Consequently it is held the said provision of Chapter 15 rul of the Calendar has not been rightly invoked after Ihe declaration of result As the jurisdiction referred in Chapler 15 Rule this stagee was not available to the Board therefore the sheet In the nature of show cause notice as well as the pruceedings and consequcnt 0FdLF3 passed by the Disciplinary Cummittee and the Appeal Cummittee, having its basis un wrongful assumption of jurisdiction, are without jurisdiction; hence the same declared Iu have been issued without any lawful authority 0f n0 legal effect the matter is decided legal ground, therefore, the factual aspect of the has not been commented upon in this order 10.

intent and not sustain in the eye of law. For what has been discussed above, the afore referred oruer dated 29.12,2018 passed Discipline Committee and order dated 05،U3 2O19 passed by Appeal Cummittee aree sut aside The instant petition is allowed accordingly dasti on payment of usual charges (MUZAMIL AKHTAR SHABIR) JUDGE '٨٧5. ٥'٥"، غت DO٧ۓ churge charg = and bein 1آر0 5 Copy
